_metadata.source: GOV.UK Immigration Rules – Appendix Skilled Worker
_metadata.notes: Rules effective from 4 April 2024 following Statement of Changes HC590

sponsorship.description: Applicant must have a valid Certificate of Sponsorship (CoS) from a UK-licensed sponsor
sponsorship.details: The sponsor must hold a valid Skilled Worker sponsor licence. The CoS must be assigned before the application is submitted. It must not have been used in a previous successful application.

occupation.description: The job must be an eligible occupation at RQF level 3 or above
occupation.details: The occupation must appear in the Immigration Salary List (Appendix Skilled Occupations). The job title alone is not sufficient; it must correspond to the correct SOC code.

salary.details: From April 2024 the general threshold is £38,700/year. New entrants pay the lower of £30,960/year or 70% of the going rate. The salary paid must meet or exceed BOTH the general threshold AND the going rate for the specific SOC code.
new_entrant_criteria: Under 26 years of age at time of application
Applying for a role where the going rate is less than £38,700
Working towards a recognised professional qualification

accepted_evidence: Passport or travel document issued by majority English-speaking country (Australia, Canada, New Zealand, USA, etc.)
GCSE, A-level, Scottish Higher in English Language/Literature (UK, Channel Islands, Isle of Man)
UK Bachelor's, Master's or PhD degree taught in English
Approved English language test (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL iBT, PTE Academic) – minimum B1 CEFR
Academic qualification from majority English-speaking country at degree level or above

points.details: 60 mandatory points are awarded for holding a valid CoS, working in an eligible occupation at the correct skill level, and meeting the salary requirement. An additional 10 points must come from tradeable characteristics (e.g., shortage occupation, PhD, STEM PhD).

financial.exempt_if: Sponsor certifies maintenance on CoS (A-rated sponsors only)
financial.details: Applicant must hold at least £1,270 in personal savings for 28 consecutive days. This requirement is waived if the sponsor is A-rated and certifies maintenance on the CoS.

criminal_record.description: No specific criminal record bar unless the applicant has received a custodial sentence of 12 months or more, is a persistent offender, or has committed a serious crime

immigration_history.description: No current ban from UK or outstanding visa overstay violations

shortage_occupations.description: Occupations on the Immigration Salary List where salary threshold is reduced to 80% of going rate
